questions.question: What is the main organizational difference between the Options API and the Composition API?
options: Options API uses classes; Composition API uses plain functions
Options API organizes code by option type (data, methods, computed); Composition API organizes by logical concern
Options API only works in Vue 2; Composition API is Vue 3 only
Options API requires TypeScript; Composition API works with plain JavaScript

questions.question: What is a composable in the context of the Composition API?
options: A special Vue component that wraps other components
A Pinia store module that holds shared state
A plain function that returns reactive state and can be reused across components
A built-in Vue directive that replaces `v-if` and `v-for`

questions.question: In a large component with search, pagination, and filters, what problem does the Options API create?
options: It forces all logic into a single `data()` function with no separation
Logic for a single feature gets scattered across `data`, `computed`, `methods`, and `watch`
It cannot use TypeScript types for reactive properties
It re-renders the entire component tree on every state change
